senses.definition: A medical condition in which the external ear is absent or stuck down because it did not form normally before birth.
examples: The child was diagnosed with ankylotia shortly after birth.
Surgery may be used to improve hearing in patients with ankylotia.
Ankylotia is a rare condition that usually affects both ears.

senses.definition: Relating to or affected by ankylosis, a stiff or fused joint or bone.
examples: The X-ray showed ankylotic changes in the patient’s spine.
Ankylotic joints can greatly limit movement and cause pain.
The dentist noted an ankylotic tooth that would be hard to remove.

senses.definition: A surgical instrument used to cut stiff or fused tissue in a joint or other ankylosed structure.
examples: The surgeon selected an ankylotome to release the fused joint.
Specialized tools such as an ankylotome are required for this delicate procedure.
Use of an ankylotome can help restore some movement in ankylosed joints.

senses.definition: A surgical operation to cut and release a stiff or fused joint or other ankylosed structure.
examples: Ankylotomy may be recommended when conservative treatment fails.
The patient underwent ankylotomy of the temporomandibular joint.
Modern imaging helps surgeons plan an ankylotomy more safely.

senses.definition: A medical condition in which the urethra is abnormally fixed to nearby tissues and cannot move freely.
examples: Ankylurethria may develop after severe infection or injury.
The surgeon noted ankylurethria during the urological examination.
Painful urination can be a symptom of ankylurethria.

senses.definition: Resembling ankylosis, especially by being stiff or abnormally fixed.
examples: The joint showed an ankyroid stiffness after the injury.
Ankyroid changes were observed in several vertebrae.
Such ankyroid deformities can reduce the patient’s mobility.

senses.definition: A short medieval dagger with a broad, double-edged blade that narrows to a point.
examples: The knight wore an anlace at his belt.
A finely decorated anlace was displayed in the museum case.
She drew the anlace in one swift motion.

senses.definition: Plural of anlace: short medieval daggers with broad, double-edged blades.
examples: The guards stood with their anlaces drawn.
Several jeweled anlaces lay on the table.
Collectors often seek anlaces from the 14th century.

senses.definition: A part of a living organism in its earliest stage that can grow into a complete organ or structure.
examples: The researcher studied the heart anlage in the embryo.
Damage to the anlage can prevent the organ from forming properly.
Each limb begins as a small anlage before it develops fully.

senses.definition: An inborn tendency or potential in a person that may develop later.
examples: Some psychologists speak of an anlage for musical ability.
He showed an anlage for leadership even as a child.
An anlage does not guarantee that a talent will appear without practice.

senses.definition: Plural of anlage: early parts of organisms that can grow into complete organs or structures.
examples: The scientist compared the limb anlagen in several species.
These anlagen will form the main parts of the nervous system.
Genetic damage can affect multiple anlagen at once.

senses.definition: Plural of anlage, meaning early forms of organs or inborn tendencies that may develop later.
examples: The embryos showed defects in several anlages.
Different anlages respond differently to environmental stress.
The study explored genetic anlages for certain diseases.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of anlace: a short medieval dagger with a broad, tapering blade.
examples: He fastened an anlas to his belt before leaving.
The old poem mentions an anlas of fine steel.
An anlas hung beside the soldier’s sword.

senses.definition: Plural of anlas: short medieval daggers with broad, tapering blades.
examples: They drew their anlases in defense.
Several ornate anlases were displayed in the hall.
The guards’ anlases flashed in the torchlight.

senses.definition: The first sound of a syllable or word, especially in studies of the German language.
examples: In this exercise, students compare the anlaut of different words.
The anlaut in German can often tell you how a word is pronounced.
Linguists examined changes in anlaut over time.

senses.definition: Plural form of anlaut, meaning the first sounds of syllables or words.
examples: Children practiced matching pictures with their correct anlaute.
The chart lists common German anlaute for beginners.
Teachers often introduce anlaute before focusing on full words.

senses.definition: A small heraldic charge shaped like a duck seen from above, used in coats of arms.
examples: The shield bore three silver anlets.
An anlet can symbolize speed or travel in heraldry.
The designer added an anlet to the family crest.

senses.definition: A rare or possibly erroneous form whose meaning is not established in contemporary English.
examples: The term anlia appears only in a few historical records.
Modern dictionaries typically do not list anlia.
The editor removed the unclear word anlia from the text.

senses.definition: A misspelling or extremely rare form, probably intended to mean anemia or another similar medical term.
examples: The student wrote anmia instead of anemia in the report.
Because anmia is not a standard term, the doctor corrected the notes.
Dictionaries usually do not recognize anmia as a valid word.

senses.definition: A female given name.
examples: Ann arrived early for the meeting.
My aunt Ann lives in the countryside.
Ann studied French at university.

senses.definition: A former coin and unit of money used in India and some neighboring countries, worth one sixteenth of a rupee.
examples: In the past, an anna was a small but useful coin.
The old contract mentions payment of eight annas.
Collectors still trade in anna coins from the colonial period.

senses.definition: A female given name.
examples: Anna moved to London last year.
I met Anna at the conference.
Anna speaks three languages fluently.

senses.definition: A female given name.
examples: Annabel will host the event this evening.
I have known Annabel since childhood.
Annabel is applying for several graduate programs.

senses.definition: A green, nickel-containing mineral that often forms as a crust or coating on other rocks.
examples: The geologist identified the bright green mineral as annabergite.
Annabergite usually appears as a surface alteration on nickel ores.
Collectors prize fine crystals of annabergite.

senses.definition: Plural of annabergite, referring to samples or occurrences of that green nickel mineral.
examples: The museum holds several annabergites from different regions.
Annabergites are often associated with other nickel ores.
Researchers compared annabergites from two mining districts.

senses.definition: A written record of events arranged in the order they happened, often year by year.
examples: The historian discovered a lost annal from the 12th century.
Each annal in the series covers one year of the king’s reign.
The monastery produced an annal that chronicled local events.

senses.definition: A rare variant form of annal meaning a historical record of events, usually arranged by year.
examples: The old text refers to an annale kept by the court scribes.
Only one annale from that period has survived.
Scholars debate how accurate the annale really is.

senses.definition: A Latin or rare plural form related to annals, referring to sets of yearly historical records.
examples: The researcher examined the annalia of several Roman writers.
These annalia provide valuable evidence for the period.
Only fragments of the original annalia remain.

senses.definition: A rare or specialized term, possibly a chemical name, whose meaning is not established in general English use.
examples: Anniline, sometimes confused with annaline, appears in older texts.
The compound called annaline is not well documented.
Because annaline is unclear, the editor added a footnote.

senses.definition: To record events year by year in the style of annals.
examples: The monks annalise the major events of each year.
He set out to annalise the reign of the emperor.
Local historians annalise changes in the town’s population.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of annalise: recorded in the form of annals.
examples: The events were annalised by the royal scribes.
Carefully annalised wars fill the manuscript.
The dynasty’s history was annalised year by year.

senses.definition: Third person singular of annalise: records events year by year in annal form.
examples: The text annalises every major battle of the period.
She annalises the kingdom’s history in great detail.
This chronicle annalises both political and religious events.

senses.definition: Present participle of annalise: recording events year by year in annals.
examples: The monks are annalising the events of the current year.
By annalising the war, they created a valuable source.
Annalising every small event takes patience.

senses.definition: A method of writing history as a simple list of events in the order they happened, usually year by year.
examples: The early chronicles show a style of annalism rather than analysis.
Critics say that pure annalism offers little explanation.
Annalism dominated medieval historical writing.

senses.definition: A historian who writes annals, recording events in the order they happened, often year by year.
examples: The medieval annalist carefully noted the dates of each event.
As an annalist, she focused on accuracy rather than style.
The work of the annalist is crucial for understanding early history.

senses.definition: Written or arranged like annals, as a simple list of events in time order.
examples: The text has an annalistic structure, year by year.
Her account of the war is mostly annalistic and lacks commentary.
Annalistic writing often begins each entry with the date.

senses.definition: In a way that follows the style of annals, listing events in simple time order.
examples: The events are annalistically arranged with little explanation.
She writes annalistically, moving from year to year.
The data are presented annalistically rather than thematically.

senses.definition: Plural of annalist: historians who write annals in time order.
examples: Medieval annalists often worked in monasteries.
The annalists recorded wars, famines, and royal deaths.
Later writers relied heavily on earlier annalists.

senses.definition: American spelling of annalise: to record events in the style of annals, year by year.
examples: The author set out to annalize the whole conflict.
They annalize the dynasty’s history in great detail.
Monks annalize important events for future readers.

senses.definition: Past simple and past participle of annalize: recorded in the form of annals.
examples: The wars were annalized by contemporary historians.
Every major event was annalized in the records.
The period is well annalized in surviving manuscripts.

senses.definition: Third person singular of annalize: records events in annal form.
examples: The chronicle annalizes each year in a few lines.
He annalizes the emperor’s campaigns without comment.
The book annalizes the kingdom’s rise and fall.

senses.definition: Present participle of annalize: recording events year by year as annals.
examples: Scribes spent their lives annalizing the affairs of the court.
Annalizing every event can be a slow process.
By annalizing the crisis, they preserved crucial details.

senses.definition: Historical records of events written in the order they happened, often year by year.
examples: The annals of the city mention a great fire in 1620.
According to the annals, the king died without an heir.
These annals are an important source for historians.

senses.definition: The recorded history or story of a field, group, or place.
examples: Her achievement will go down in the annals of science.
The club’s annals are full of colorful characters.
It is a legendary moment in the annals of sport.

senses.definition: A place name, used especially in historical or local Irish contexts.
examples: The clan once ruled the territory of Annaly.
Annaly appears in several medieval Irish documents.
They traced their family back to Annaly in the 15th century.

senses.definition: A former name used during the French colonial period for a central region of what is now Vietnam.
examples: Under French rule, Annam was one of the regions of Indochina.
Many documents from that time refer to central Vietnam as Annam.
The history of Annam is closely linked to that of modern Vietnam.

senses.definition: Relating to Annam or its people and language; now considered old-fashioned and sometimes offensive.
examples: The colonial report refers to Annamese customs and laws.
Annamese culture was described from a foreign perspective.
Old texts often use the term Annamese for Vietnamese people.

senses.definition: A person from Annam; now old-fashioned and sometimes offensive.
examples: The author describes an Annamese working in the colonial administration.
Several Annamese were interviewed for the study.
The word Annamese has largely been replaced by Vietnamese.

senses.definition: Relating to Annam; now old-fashioned and often considered offensive.
examples: Old maps show the Annamite coast in central Vietnam.
The Annamite mountains run along the border region.
Colonial documents mention various Annamite communities.

senses.definition: A person from Annam; now old-fashioned and often considered offensive.
examples: The novel portrays an Annamite who works for the French.
Many Annamites moved to the cities during that time.
The term Annamite is rarely used in modern English.

senses.definition: Relating to Annam or its language and people; now a rare and old-fashioned term.
examples: The book includes a short Annamitic vocabulary list.
Annamitic folktales were translated into French.
He studied the Annamitic language while in Indochina.

senses.definition: The capital city of the U.S. state of Maryland, known for the United States Naval Academy.
examples: They drove to Annapolis for the weekend.
Annapolis is famous for its historic harbor.
He studied engineering at the Naval Academy in Annapolis.

senses.definition: A group of high mountains in the Himalayas in central Nepal, including several peaks over 7,000 meters.
examples: They planned a trekking route around Annapurna.
Annapurna is known for its challenging climbing routes.
The view of Annapurna at sunrise was unforgettable.

senses.definition: A very rare or obsolete term whose meaning is not clear in modern English.
examples: The glossary lists annard but does not define it.
Because annard is obscure, most readers will not recognize it.
The editor chose to replace annard with a clearer word.

senses.definition: Relating to a year or to records arranged by year, similar to annal.
examples: The text follows an annary structure.
These annary records list events for each year.
The annary style reflects the concerns of medieval writers.
